english: artificial, feigned, fictitious, ma-bcós (ma bcos) artless, unaffected, genuine; it also seems to de-note an absence of mental activity, or a forbearance of exercising such activity, in short that indifference to the world, which is so highly valued by the Buddhist, Mil. — bcós-pai ras (bcos pai ras), or ras bcós-bu (ras bcos bu), washed or prepared cotton-cloth Cs.; calico, chintz Cs.; in S. O. it seems to denote a costly, va-luable fabric; bcós-ma (bcos ma) sbst. and adj., a production of art, any thing made or contrived by art, esp. every thing imitated, counterfeit, mock, sham, not genuine, frq.; bcós-ma ma yin-pa (bcos ma ma yin pa) natural, unfeigned, ge-nuine, e.g. respect, reverence Glr. — tsúl-bcós-mk̥an (tsul bcos mkhan), one that is shamming, a hypocrite. Cf. °c̥ós-pa ('chos pa).
Ld. for lci-ba (lci ba), excrement, dung, manure.
= bcá-sga (bca' sga), white ginger, v. sgá (sga).
1. Cs.: a sort of carrot, Med. frq., but not known to the common people, at least not in W. — 2. कम्बल acc. to Was. a garment made of wool or felt Tar.

1. rod, switch, stick, whip; glaṅ-lcág (glang lcag) ox-whip; rṅa-lcág (rnga lcag) kettle-drum stick; lcaṅ-lcóg (lcang lcog) Lex. willow-twig, osier-switch; rta-lcág (rta lcag) horse-whip, whip in ge-neral, also a scourge, consisting of several straps with sharp knots; spa-lcág (spa lcag) a cane, bamboo Mil.; ber(-ma)-lcág (ber (-ma) lcag) stick Mil. — 2. (lcág-ma (lcag ma)) stroke, blow, cut, hit, lcág rgyáb-pa (lcag rgyab pa) to give a blow or cut, rtá-la (rta la) to the horse Glr.; mgo-lcág (mgo lcag) (Ld. *go-lcág* (*go lcag*)) a blow or stroke upon the head; °gram-lcág ('gram lcag) a smack on the cheek, slap on the face, box on the ear Cs.; t̥al-lcág (thal lcag) id. — 3. fore-part of a coat of mail Sch. — 4. a kind of Daphne, v. re-lcág-pa (re lcag pa). Comp. lcág-rdo (lcag rdo) W. flint, flint-stone. — lcág-brás (lcag bras) Mil. whip-cord, lash of a whip; lcág-brén (lcag bren), and lcág-dṅó (lcag dngo) id. — lcág-ts̥án (lcag tshan) = rta-lcág (rta lcag) C. — lcág-yú (lcag yu) whip-stick, handle of a whip.
a girdle, made of plaited and interlaced strips and resembling a chain; one Lex. adds: dáṅ-mai °dril-du lhás-pa (dang mai 'dril du lhas pa) (?).

1. iron, lcágs-kyi (lcags kyi) of iron; lcágs-bton-mk̥an (lcags bton mkhan) a miner digging for iron; rgya-lcágs (rgya lcags) Chinese iron; p̥o-lcágs (pho lcags) an in-ferior sort of iron, mo-lcágs (mo lcags) a finer and better sort of it, Cs. steel (?) — 2. an iron instrument, tool, esp. lock (of doors), fet-ter, shackle, sgo t̥ams-cád lcágs btab-ciṅ (sgo thams cad lcags btab cing) locking every door Pth.; *káṅ-cag lág-cag* (*kang cag lag cag*) C. fettered on hands and feet; rnam-lcágs (rnam lcags) 1. thunderbolt, 2. a flash of lightning just striking an object; me-lcágs (me lcags) a steel to strike fire with, fire-steel. Comp. and deriv. lcágs-kyú (lcags kyu) B. an iron hook, esp. fishing-hook, angle; often fig.: t̥úgs-rjei (thugs rjei), or c̥ós-kyi lcágs-kyús °dzín-pa (chos kyi lcags kyus 'dzin pa) to seize with the hook of grace or of religion Dzl., Glr. and elsewh. — lcágs-dkár (lcags dkar) tin-plate, white iron plate. — lcágs-skúd (lcags skud) thin wire. — lcágs-k̥yém (lcags khyem) or k̥yém (khyem) a spade. — lcágs-k̥ról (lcags khrol) Sch. a big iron kettle (= W. *°cag-dol* (*'cag dol*) stew-pan, large iron pan or pot?) — lcágs-mgár (lcags mgar) iron smith, black-smith. — lcágs-sgór (lcags sgor) iron pan. — lcágs-sgyíd (lcags sgyid) trevet, tripod. — lcágs-sgróg (lcags sgrog) fetter, shackle. — lcágs-c̥ás (lcags chas) implements of iron, hardware. — lcágs-tíg (lcags tig) a kind of gentian, cf. tig-ta (tig ta). — lcágs-t̥ág (lcags thag) chain or chains. — lcágs-t̥ál (lcags thal) Cs. an iron dish or plate, prob. from t̥á-li (tha li). — lcágs-drégs (lcags dregs) (W. *°cag-rág* (*'cag rag*)) 1. iron dross, scoria or slag of iron; 2. dirt of the in-testines. — lcágs-rdó (lcags rdo) 1. perh. more cor-rectly lcág-rdó (lcag rdo) flint-stone. 2. iron-stone, iron ore (?). — lcágs-p̥rá (lcags phra) Ü, a kind of musket, imported from Rum (Turkey). — *°cag-bér* (*'cag ber*) W. an iron bar, crowbar, hand-spike. — lcágs-mag (lcags mag), bcá-mag (bca' mag), the Turkish چکماک flint-stone, tinder-box W. — lcágs-ts̥ágs (lcags tshags) an iron cribble or sieve, colander. — lcágs-zám (lcags zam) iron bridge. — lcágs-záṅs (lcags zangs) iron kettle. — *°cag-záṅ* (*'cag zang*) C. good iron, steel. — lcágs-yyá (lcags g.ya') rust Med. — lcágs-rí (lcags ri) a wall en-circling an estate, a town etc. — lcágs-sláṅ (lcags slang) a large iron pan for roasting or kiln-
